subject: Re: Warp reconstruction using external .xf
body:
Hi Xiaofeng,

When you say "I couldn't get correctly aligned tomograms" which of the
following do you mean?
1. the contents do not overlay directly between IMOD generated tomograms
and Warp generated tomograms
2. the tomogram appears poorly aligned in Warp generated tomograms but
correct in IMOD generated tomograms.

General advice is to always pick particles in tomograms generated by the
package/pipeline you will use for high res refinements. If 1. you will need
to either repick or figure out the appropriate transformations to apply to
you particles for each tilt series.

If 2. then there may be some issue.

> And I have other questions about building up a windows workstation:

If you have access to linux based computing resources I would hold off on
buying a windows machine for Warp/M for now

Cheers,

Alister


On Sat, 4 May 2024 at 13:28, Xiaofeng CHU <chuxiaofeng123@gmail.com> wrote:

> Dear Warp community,
>
> I met some trouble when using Warp to reconstruct the tomograms.
> If you would have time to take a look I'll be very appreciated.
>
> The issue arises when attempting to reconstruct tomograms using Warp
> (1.10).
> Initially, I processed the tilt-series alignment in Imod, and processed
> them through the standard STA pipeline.
> Now, to improve resolution using M, to do this I need to reconstruct the
> same tilt-series in Warp.
> I would like to use the .xf file generated in Imod to ensure consistency
> in particle coordinates.
> .xf file and taSolution were copied into "imod/tomogramXXX" folder
> generated by "import tilt series from imod", and followed by "full-tomogram
> reconstruction".
> In the result, I couldn't get correctly aligned tomograms. But I could get
> correct reconstructions by executing "tilt" in imod using the exact same
> files (.st.mrc and .xf).
> Do you have any idea where could go wrong?
>
> And I have other questions about building up a windows workstation:
> We would like to buy a workstation for warp and M. Could you please give
> me a few advice about the configurations of the workstation (less than
> €2000)?
> For example, which kind of  GPU would you recommend?  Do multiple GPUs
> help for acceleration? Does gaming GPU (Geforce) work? Does cpu from AMD
> work?
>
> Thank you so much for your time!
> Best,
> Xiaofeng
>
> --

> .
>
